Q: Are the procedures (ex: notices, public hearings, resolutions) for creating a town TID under state law (sec. 60.23, Wis. Stats.), different from those for a city or village?
A: No. The procedures are the same because the town chose to exercise all powers of a city, under sec. 66.1105, Wis. Stats. See theTID Checklist.

Q: What additional requirements must a town meet compared to a city or village when creating a TID under sec. 60.23, Wis. Stats.?
State law (sec.60.23(32)(f)2., Wis. Stats.) requires the town to select option (a), (b) or (c). What impact do these three options have on the project plan?
Is debt service included in the timeframe for Option (b) or Option (c)?
A: Option (b) —the creation project plan must demonstrate the TID will have sufficient revenue to pay off debt and all project costs within 90% of the TID's maximum life. After all project costs are paid, the TID must terminate.

Q: Does a town have to choose option (a), (b) or (c) each time it amends a TID, or do the options only apply when creating a TID?
A: A town must select an option (a, b, or c) when creating the TID, and document the selection in the project plan.  If the town completes an amendment, it must reconfirm the TID's original creation option or select a different option.

Q: What limit percentage affects a town?
A: 12% —if the TID is created under sec. 60.23, Wis. Stats., the 12% limit is used because the town chose to exercise all powers of a city under sec. 66.1105, Wis. Stats.

Q: Can a town TID still be created under sec. 60.85, Wis. Stats.?
A: Yes. A town may create a TID under sec. 60.85, Wis. Stats. For more information, review theTown Creationweb page.
